original_hypothesis.title: TaikoL2EIP1559Configurable Owner Can Manipulate Gas Pricing to Extreme Values
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: setConfigAndExcess allows the owner to set gasExcess to type(uint64).max, causing the exponential basefee calculation to reach astronomical levels, making all L2 transactions prohibitively expensive. Alternatively, setting gasExcess to 0 permanently disables dynamic fees (basefee = 1 wei). The owner can also set basefeeAdjustmentQuotient to extreme values to distort the fee curve.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: While the function has basic validation (gasTargetPerL1Block != 0, basefeeAdjustmentQuotient != 0), there are no bounds on gasExcess. The owner (cross-chain via bridge) could set values that create extreme economic conditions for all L2 users. This is an admin abuse scenario but the impact is protocol-critical.

proof_of_signal.pattern: No bounds check on _newGasExcess; can be set to 0 (disable) or type(uint64).max (extreme basefee)

preconditions: Owner account (or cross-chain owner via bridge) is compromised
Owner calls setConfigAndExcess with extreme parameters

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: gasExcess = type(uint64).max → Lib1559Math.basefee returns maximum exponential value
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Owner sets gasExcess to 0 (disable fees) or max (prohibitive fees)
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Extreme basefee manipulation affects all L2 users immediately

economic_questions: What is the maximum basefee when gasExcess = type(uint64).max?
How quickly can the owner be stopped via governance?
What value bounds would be reasonable for gasExcess?
validation_requirements: Assess governance response time for emergency parameter changes
Model economic impact on L2 users under extreme fee scenarios

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Indirect: economic disruption of entire L2

potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Transactions become impossible (too expensive) or fee-free (spam risk)

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Cross-chain ownership security and governance response time
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Basefee calculation at extreme gasExcess values

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
